gauge.name: Soquel Creek at Soquel

impacts.statement: Major Flooding.  Floods of record roughly equivalent to this level (22.33 feet in 1955; 21.85 feet in 1982).  Debris blockage at Soquel Drive Bridge possible, as well as major backwatering in downtown Soquel.  Capitola and Esplanade flooded, with widespread regional damage.

Moderate Flooding.  Flooding of several feet in downtown Soquel, Old Mill area, Heart of Soquel, and houses along Soquel Wharf Road flooded, and other areas along creek in Capitola and Esplanade.  Soquel Drive Bridge likely backwatered.

Minor Flooding.  Water begins to reach riverfront properties in downtown Soquel, including the Old Mill mobile home park, Heart of Soquel, and along Soquel Wharf Road.  Flooding may also be possible downstream in Capitola, especially during high tide.  NWS will issue a Flash Flood Warning, if not yet already issued.
Monitor Stage.  Three feet below flood stage.  NWS may issue a Flash Flood Watch, if not yet already issued.

dataAttribution.text: USGS--Water Resources of the United States
dataAttribution.title: US Geological Survey
Observations courtesy of U.S. Geological Survey

gauge.forecastReliability: Graphical forecasts are not available during times of high water, forecast crest information can be found in the local WFO web page.
